note: Machine-checked status boundary for the Surface-derived epistemic invariants. enforced-slice means the cited subsystem is tested but the general ResearchRun integration remains open.

invariants.title: Human objective authority
invariants.description: Human-owned state cannot be agent-mutated; general research-objective authority remains to be assembled.

tests.contains: agent cannot mutate the human/video layer directly

invariants.closure: Bind the objective and every material scope expansion to a human authority event in ResearchRun.

invariants.title: Source isolation and explicit ingress
invariants.description: Private tool state crosses into the shared record only through a bounded artifact interface.
invariants.closure: Implement source manifests, content digests, access/license boundaries, and host-only artifact ingress.

invariants.title: Provisional generation
invariants.description: Generated layers carry ownership and provenance and remain shakeable.

tests.contains: generated layers carry ownership and provenance

invariants.closure: Generalize provisional/retractable status from scene layers to hypotheses, reports, and research artifacts.

invariants.title: Bounded autonomous intervals
invariants.description: The flow channel enforces angle, lifetime, cumulative budget, and closure before violation.

tests.contains: closes before a frame can exceed the interval budget

invariants.closure: Extend channel scope to research sources, tools, persistence, network, perturbation, and publication boundaries.

invariants.title: Scheduler neutrality
invariants.description: STEP and FLOW schedule the same event transition and yield identical traces.

tests.contains: gives STEP and FLOW scheduling identical traces

invariants.title: Complete provenance and replay
invariants.description: Stage mutations are audited and flow traces export, digest, replay, and reject forgery.

tests.contains: detects a forged accepted mutation against the human layer
records an audit event for an accepted mutation

invariants.closure: Unify cross-tool inputs, versions, seeds, costs, artifacts, and verdicts in one canonical research trace.

invariants.title: Typed epistemic status
invariants.description: Lean and source ledgers validate distinct proof, model, condition, and obligation statuses.
invariants.closure: Define a shared claim-status vocabulary spanning sources, hypotheses, observations, runtime results, theorems, interpretations, acceptance, and debt.

invariants.title: Challenge before promotion
invariants.description: Tracked witnesses retain negative controls and countermodels, but challenge coverage is not yet universal.
invariants.closure: Require every positive ResearchRun result to name executed challenges or emit explicit challenge debt.

invariants.title: Dependency-aware revocation
invariants.description: Flow interruption closes the channel, preserves the human signal, and cascades through dependents.
invariants.closure: Apply invalidation, downgrade, or recheck semantics to the general source and claim dependency graph.

invariants.title: No autonomous closure or actuation
invariants.description: Gatekeeper and witness boundaries prevent direct authority over protected state; general external actuation remains outside the runtime.

tests.contains: rejects a proposal that targets the human layer
rejects a step the gate admitted against a refused certificate

invariants.closure: Represent promotion, scope expansion, publication, deployment, messaging, spending, and external mutation as separate human-authorized events.
